claim: Lost person behavior can be modeled as a random variable drawing from a distribution of 6 known reorientation strategies; fitting these distributions to real incident data produces an empirically validated behavioral profile that predicts probable location over time.
stance_target: Bayesian/probabilistic behavioral prior as the correct framework for lost-entity location estimation
scope_conditions: Hashimoto et al. 2022 Scientific Reports; validated against ISRID (International SAR Incident Database); human lost-person behavior; adaptation to dogs requires domain-specific behavioral distribution fitting
quoted_support: The behavior random variable selects from a distribution of six known lost person reorientation strategies to simulate the agent's trajectory. We systematically simulate a range of possible behavior distributions and find a best-fit behavioral profile for a hiker with the International Search and Rescue Incident Database.
numbers: 46,609 people lost in US National Parks 2004–2014
entities: International Search and Rescue Incident Database (ISRID)

claim: As time passes after a lost-entity event, the probability distribution of location expands, survival probability declines, and the area to be searched grows — establishing temporal urgency as a first-order variable in lost-subject operations.
stance_target: temporal decay in recovery probability as a key driver of search prioritization
scope_conditions: Hashimoto 2022 SAR context; wilderness humans; analogous principle applies to lost dogs
quoted_support: As time passes, the search area grows, survival rate decreases, and searchers are faced with an increasingly daunting task of searching large areas in a short amount of time.

claim: The ISRID behavioral database is the primary empirical source for lost-person reorientation strategy distributions; Koester's work (2008 book + this 2022 model) represents the state of the art in SAR behavioral prior construction.
stance_target: existence of validated empirical priors for SAR behavioral modeling that can anchor analogous lost-dog priors
scope_conditions: Hashimoto 2022; human SAR; ISRID is the authoritative database
quoted_support: We validate these results with a leave-one-out analysis. This work represents the first time-discrete model of lost person dynamics validated with data from real SAR incidents.
